purpose: Minimal machine-readable current context and routing for agents.

do_not_read_by_default: old merge handoff files unless explicitly relevant
read_policy.history_read_condition: Only read logs/archive when explicitly asked for history, debugging a repeated known error, regression archaeology, or old audit details.

context_policy.routine_work: Do not update docs/logs/state for routine actions unless user asks or the task changes architecture, deploy, env, security, billing, retention, or agent routing.
context_policy.errors_log: Append to .agents/logs/errors.jsonl only for sanitized reusable root-cause/fix knowledge.

repo_state.latest_origin_dev_deploy_seen: 5251018 vkbot: remove trailing dots from model menus
repo_state.note: At update time serega contains work newer than origin/main. Use normal protected PR/approval flow before treating main as updated.

current_focus.summary: Continue product work on serega while keeping dev-deploy and main release flows aligned. DEV is the daily integration/testing contour; main is production and deploys through GitHub Actions with approval/protection.

active_environment_model.development: dev-deploy branch and DEV VPS/Cloudflare/VK community. DEV should mirror production architecture but use DEV-specific secrets, tunnel, VK group and payment/provider settings.
active_environment_model.production: main branch only. Production deploy is GitHub Actions -> GHCR images -> VPS deploy script -> smoke -> rollback if needed.
active_environment_model.loadtest: APP_ENV=loadtest with mock providers/payments/delivery only. Do not use production hosts or paid providers in load tests.

recent_completed_work: Security/scale hardening and local/mock k6 smoke baseline were merged from fastlife_dev into the active project history.
DEV deploy flow exists separately from production deploy and can be triggered from dev-deploy.
Data retention, analytics aggregate, data services contract, and local/external/managed data-service modes were added.
Video/image provider catalog was narrowed to active public runtime surfaces; provider/model exposure is controlled by route/catalog flags and env.
VK bot payment copy and menu copy were polished; default starting balance was reduced to 30 via latest serega work.
active_priorities: Before production release, merge serega to main through the protected PR/review flow; do not force-push main.
Keep DEV and PROD env files synchronized by variable names, but never copy DEV secret values into PROD.
Verify production PROD_ENV_FILE before deploy when new provider/payment/feature flags are added.
Run live YooKassa smoke before real-money confidence: payment.succeeded, payment.canceled, refund.succeeded, replay/idempotency and reconciliation.
Confirm public YooKassa webhook route goes to cmd/provider-webhook/PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_ADDR, not cmd/api.
Finish capacity/load report with p95/p99, CPU/RAM, Redis queue depth, Postgres locks/slow queries and DLQ before claiming scale readiness.
Plan external/managed Postgres, Redis and S3 migration before serious traffic; current code supports modes, but operational migration must be rehearsed.
Keep provider calls isolated in worker/adapters; VK bot, Mini App and cmd/api must not call AI providers directly.
open_risks: DEV/PROD drift can happen if DEV_ENV_FILE and PROD_ENV_FILE are edited manually without a variable-name comparison.
Real provider/payment smoke tests can spend money or touch external systems; require explicit approval and correct environment.
Load tests currently prove mock/local behavior, not real provider throughput or external quota limits.
Lot/FIFO attribution is still needed before automatic or partial refunds for already-spent credits.
Old handoff docs may contain stale branch names or outdated priorities; prefer this state file for current routing.
security_invariants: No secrets, tokens, auth headers, full launch params, prompt bodies, provider payloads, private artifact URLs or raw PII in git, logs, docs or chat.
VK handlers, Mini App BFF and cmd/api never call AI providers directly.
Billing remains append-only ledger based; no direct balance mutation.
Payment redirect/confirmation URL is not proof of payment.
YooKassa top-up credits only through provider-verified webhook/reconciliation plus ledger.
Do not weaken auth, signature checks, moderation, billing, idempotency, TLS, webhook trust or production CORS.

subagents.input: Minimal goal, allowed files/packages, forbidden actions, output schema.
subagents.output: Technical schema-only: status, findings file:line issue fix, changed_files/tests, residual_risks.
subagents.owner: Main agent owns decisions, integration, code quality and user-facing report.
